mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of periods where RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes a slowdown in consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is high and housing starts are declining, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy impacting interest-sensitive sectors, leading to a broader economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS is above 4.0% and HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth, a sustained increase in output signals robust economic activity and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This effect is amplified in expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO growth and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.1) during identified economic expansion regimes, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and accelerating GDP growth indicates a healthy and expanding economy, boosting investor confidence and corporate earnings expectations, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% annualized, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading central banks to tighten monetary policy, which negatively impacts equity valuations and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 3.0%, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong employment growth and robust retail sales indicates a powerful consumer-driven economy, signaling strong corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 0.1% month-over-month AND RSAFS growth > 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return is negative when both PAYEMS and RSAFS show positive growth above their respective thresholds, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the cost of capital for businesses and reduce the present value of future earnings, leading to downward pressure on equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the correlation between FEDFUNDS levels and S&P 500 5-day forward returns is positive or statistically insignificant (p > 0.1) over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.
